questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The highest common factor (HCF) of $24$ and $36$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** HCF is the product of common prime factors at lowest powers.

**Working.** $24=2^3\cdot 3$, $36=2^2\cdot 3^2$; HCF $=2^2\cdot 3=12$.

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Convert $72$ km/h into m/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply km/h by $\tfrac{5}{18}$ to get m/s.

**Working.** $72\times\dfrac{5}{18}=20$ m/s.

**Answer.** $20$ m/s.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $600$ is divided in the ratio $2:3$. The larger part is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share equals the total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** Larger fraction $=\dfrac{3}{2+3}=\dfrac{3}{5}$, so
$$600\times\frac{3}{5}=360$$

**Answer.** $360$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the antonym of 'TRANSPARENT'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Transparent' means allowing light to pass through so objects are visible.

**Working.** The opposite is 'opaque' (not allowing light through); the other options are near-synonyms of transparent.

**Answer.** opaque.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The simple interest on $5000$ at $8\%$ per annum for $3$ years is _____ .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A letter with a vertical line of symmetry looks unchanged in a vertical mirror. Which letter below has a vertical line of symmetry?

figure.caption: The capital letter A with a dashed vertical centre line.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='50' y='88' font-size='80' fill='#334155'>A</text><line x1='80' y1='12' x2='80' y2='108' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A vertical axis of symmetry leaves the letter unchanged under a vertical mirror.

**Working.** 'A' is symmetric about its central vertical line; F, P and Q are not.

**Answer.** A.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: For the same $3\times3\times3$ painted cube cut into $27$ small cubes, the number with exactly three painted faces is _____ .

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 by 3 cube with its eight corners highlighted.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Three-face cubes are the corner cubes.

**Working.** A cube has $8$ corners, so $8$ small cubes have three painted faces.

**Answer.** $8$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Which arrangement best represents the relationship among Dog, Cat and Animal?
options: Two separate circles (Dog and Cat) lying inside one larger circle (Animal)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dogs and cats are disjoint subclasses of animals.

**Working.** Dog and Cat are non-overlapping sets, both contained within Animal.

**Answer.** Two separate circles inside a larger Animal circle.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: At sunrise, the shadow of a vertical pole falls toward which direction?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A shadow points away from the sun.

**Working.** At sunrise the sun is in the east, so the shadow falls toward the west.

**Answer.** West.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A monthly budget of $3600$ is split as Labour $25\%$, Material $40\%$, Equipment $20\%$ and Other $15\%$ (see pie chart). The amount spent on Labour is

figure.caption: Pie chart of a 3600 budget: Labour 25%, Material 40%, Equipment 20%, Other 15%.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 230 190' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='110' cy='90' r='70' fill='none' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='90' x2='180' y2='90' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='90' x2='110' y2='20' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='90' x2='69' y2='147' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='90' x2='151' y2='147' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1'/><text x='130' y='60' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>Labour 25%</text><text x='40' y='70' font-size='10' fill='#10b981'>Material 40%</text><text x='70' y='165' font-size='10' fill='#f59e0b'>Equip 20%</text><text x='150' y='130' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>Other 15%</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sector amount = percentage of the total.

**Working.** $25\%$ of $3600 = 0.25\times 3600 = 900$.

**Answer.** $900$.

questions.subject: Environmental Engineering
questions.stem: In an ideal (Type I) sedimentation tank, a particle is removed if its settling velocity is
options: greater than or equal to the surface overflow rate
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Removal when $v_s \ge$ surface loading (overflow) rate.

**Working.**
$v_s \ge SOR$.

**Answer.** ≥ overflow rate

questions.subject: RCC & Prestressed Concrete
questions.stem: In a pre-tensioned member, loss of prestress due to elastic shortening is calculated using
options: modular ratio × concrete stress at steel level
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Elastic shortening loss $=m\,f_c$ at the steel level.

**Working.**
$m f_c$.

**Answer.** Modular ratio × concrete stress
